data.title: Openlane signals growth with $250M share repurchase plan and strong Q1 performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OPENLANE, Inc. (KAR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Peter Kelly highlighted OPENLANE's strong performance in Q1 2025, emphasizing record achievements in several areas, including a 7% increase in consolidated revenue and adjusted EBITDA of $83 million. He noted the asset-light digital operating model's scalability as a key driver for these results.
  • The company announced Brad Herring as its new Executive Vice President and CFO, effective later this month.
  • The Marketplace segment recorded a 15% growth in dealer-to-dealer volumes year-over-year, marking the second consecutive quarter of double-digit growth. This segment also saw increased new buyer and seller participation, with sales records set in the U.S. marketplace.
  • The Finance segment achieved a 15% increase in adjusted EBITDA, supported by the lowest loan-loss rate since Q4 2022 and a 2% increase in total loan transactions.
  • The Board of Directors authorized a new $250 million share repurchase program, replacing the previous $100 million authorization. This program extends through the end of 2026.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance in the range of $290 million to $310 million. CEO Kelly stated that despite market uncertainties, the company remains well-positioned for long-term growth.
  • The company expects commercial off-lease volumes to recover starting in 2026, which will contribute to future revenue growth.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated revenue for Q1 2025 was $460 million, a 7% increase year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA rose by 11% to $83 million.
  • Marketplace revenue increased 10% to $351 million, with auction fee revenue growing 14% thanks to volume growth and pricing improvements.
  • The Finance segment reported $46 million in adjusted EBITDA, driven by strong risk management and a 13.9% net finance margin.
  • The company generated $123 million in cash flow from operations during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Craig Kennison, Baird: Asked about the impact of tariffs on dealer activity and volumes. CEO Kelly explained that the strong Q1 results were largely unaffected by tariff-related activity, which only provided a slight incremental benefit late in the quarter.
  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Inquired about the percentage of Canadian vehicles exported to the U.S. under new tariff conditions. CEO Kelly responded that less than 20% of Canadian transactions typically result in exports, and most exported vehicles are exempt from tariffs.
  • Bret Jordan, Jefferies: Questioned the pricing environment and its role in share gains. CEO Kelly noted that pricing increases in Canada were effective and well-received, with no negative impact on customer perceptions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a neutral-to-positive tone, focusing on tariff impacts and market share gains. Their questions revealed curiosity about sustained volume growth and the effectiveness of pricing strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, emphasizing operational strengths and strategic initiatives. CEO Kelly repeatedly underscored the company's positioning and growth prospects, using phrases like "strong scalability" and "well-prepared for long-term growth."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the sentiment was consistent, with management continuing to project confidence in their strategic direction.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 saw a slight decline in commercial volumes, consistent with expectations, while dealer-to-dealer volumes continued to grow at double-digit rates, similar to Q4 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA growth moderated from 18% in Q4 2024 to 11% in Q1 2025, reflecting seasonal and strategic factors.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic, with a clear focus on executing long-term growth strategies.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly, with increased attention to tariff impacts and potential market adjustments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the uncertainty surrounding tariffs and their potential long-term effects on vehicle supply and values. CEO Kelly stated that the company is actively planning for multiple scenarios.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of tariffs on Canadian exports and potential headwinds for dealer activity in light of economic uncertainties.

Final Takeaway

OPENLANE, Inc. delivered robust Q1 2025 results, driven by strong performance in both the Marketplace and Finance segments. The company highlighted its scalability and asset-light model as key strengths, while reaffirming its long-term growth strategy. Notable developments included a new $250 million share repurchase program and ongoing investments in technology and customer experience. Despite challenges such as tariffs and macroeconomic uncertainties, management expressed confidence in the company's trajectory and reaffirmed its 2025 guidance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Gold falls over 1% amid trade deal hopes; ferrous market under strain
data.description:
Gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) extended losses on Thursday after reports of U.S. President Donald Trump signing a potential trade deal with Britain dampened market sentiment, while investors looked ahead to the outcome of upcoming U.S.-China trade talks this weekend.

Spot gold fell over 1% to $3,340.77 an ounce at the time of writing.

Further weighing on prices, the Federal Reserve has kept interest rates unchanged.

"It was a sea of red across commodities as news that the Federal Reserve left US interest rates on hold, and signs it's likely to remain there, overcame enthusiasm about US-China trade talks," ING analysts said.

Elsewhere, iron ore futures declined after China, the world’s top consumer, signaled possible cuts to steel production amid weak demand.

Futures fell as much as 2.3%, paring gains from Wednesday when the People’s Bank of China announced easing measures, including cutting the seven-day reverse repurchase rate. The turnaround came as the impact of tariffs and output cuts outweighed the government support measures, Bloomberg reported.

The China Iron and Steel Association said this week the government was “actively deploying and promoting” its crude steel production mandate. 

“The main task facing the steel industry is how to maintain a balance between supply and demand under limited market demand,” Wang Bin, director of CISA’s planning and development department, said at a recent press conference.

Anticipated output restrictions have pressured the market and are likely to reduce demand for iron ore.

Copper futures (HG1:COM) meanwhile, were down 1.7% against a stronger dollar (DXY). The ferrous market remained weak despite Beijing rolling out a series of monetary stimulus measures aimed at easing the economic strain from the trade war with the United States.

ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), (OTC:JJCTF).

ETFs liked to precious metals: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Crypto-linked stocks rise as Bitcoin hovers around $100K mark
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has not just regained its momentum but is also nearing the $100K mark once again, for the first time since early February. 

At the time of writing, the world's largest digital currency was up 2.9% at $99.7K. 

Bitcoin, considered a speculative asset, seemed to have recovered quite well, even as stocks have seen a volatile few months owing to tariff uncertainties and worries of potential recession.

Keep in mind that Bitcoin's record high was $108.1K, reached on Jan. 20, 2025, the day Donald Trump started his second term as U.S. president.

The cryptocurrency has traded above the $90K mark since April 22, leading to talks about it being a safe haven asset to rise. 

“Bitcoin's market share hits a four-year-high, sparking debate about its role as a safe haven asset,” Seeking Alpha analyst Dean Popplewell said.

“Bitcoin ETFs are seeing increased inflows, particularly BlackRock's IBIT, signaling potential bullish movement. Crypto regulation remains complex, with some states like New Hampshire moving forward while others stall,” Popplewell added.

BTC's rise on Thursday comes amid reports saying that U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to announce a trade agreement with the United Kingdom.

The rally lifted cryptocurrency-related stocks in premarket trading, with notable gains in MicroStrategy (MSTR) +5%, Hive Digital (HIVE) +4%, Hut 8 (HUT) +7%, Bitfarms (BITF) +3%, Cipher Mining (CIFR) +7%, Bit Digital (BTBT) +5%, Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) +5%, MARA Holdings (NASDAQ:MARA) +5%, Coinbase (COIN) +4%, CleanSpark (CLSK) +5%, and Robinhood Markets (HOOD) +3%.

data.title: CrowdStrike CEO after company layoffs: 'AI flattens our hiring curve'
data.description:
Artificial intelligence "flattens our hiring curve," CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) CEO George Kurtz told employees when announcing layoffs of about 5% of the cybersecurity firm's global workforce.

"We're operating in a market and technology inflection point, with AI reshaping every industry, accelerating threats, and evolving customer needs," Kurtz wrote in a letter to staff.

"To lead at scale, with nearly 10,000 CrowdStrikers and a clear path to $10B in ARR, we are evolving how we operate. We're building on what works, simplifying execution, and doubling down on our highest-impact opportunities," he added.

Kurtz noted that AI is a force multiplier throughout CrowdStrike's (NASDAQ:CRWD) business, as it streamlines go-to-market, improves customer outcomes, and drives efficiencies.

Jefferies analysts view the job cuts "as a result of ensuring progress on profit post-CCP (Customer Commitment Package) margin headwinds and not an indication of a slowdown in demand or growth." 

CCP was an initiative launched to reinforce customer relationships following last year's global IT outage. CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) ended the initiative in March.

Jefferies added that the company has also likely developed generative AI-based capabilities that could also allow it to do more with less headcount.

data.title: Nintendo reports results, guidance
data.description:
• Nintendo's (OTCPK:NTDOY) profit for the fiscal year through March totaled 278.8 billion yen ($1.9 billion), down from 490 billion yen the previous fiscal year.

  • Annual sales slipped 30% to 1.16 trillion yen.

  • Nintendo forecast a 300 billion yen ($2.1 billion) profit for the fiscal year ending in March next year, up nearly 8% from the fiscal year just ended, on sales of 1.9 trillion yen, a 63% on-year rise.

  • Nintendo said it expects to sell 15 million Switch 2 consoles for the fiscal year through March 2026.

  • Nintendo Switch 2 will be released on June 5, 2025.

data.title: ACM Research beats Q1 estimates, maintain guidance
